ai-e1.title: Explain JDK vs JVM vs JRE.
ai-e1.answer: **JDK** = write & compile (javac, tools). **JRE** = run apps (JVM + libraries). **JVM** = executes bytecode on any OS.
ai-e1.realWorld: Laptop has JDK for coding. Server runs your JAR with a JVM — same bytecode, different OS.

ai-e2.title: Explain variables like you're 10 years old.
ai-e2.answer: Variables store values: `int age = 12;` `String name = "Riya";` Type is fixed; you can reassign compatible values.
ai-e2.realWorld: Game stats: health, coins, playerName — updated as the program runs.

ai-e3.title: Generate a Java Hello World and explain every line.
ai-e3.answer: `public class HelloWorld` — class name. `public static void main` — entry point. `System.out.println` — print line. Braces close method and class.
ai-e3.realWorld: `java HelloWorld` → JVM runs main() → output in terminal. Every Java app starts here.

ai-e4.title: Explain why a syntax error occurs.
ai-e4.answer: Syntax error = compiler can't parse code: missing `;`, bad braces, typos. Fix the line javac reports before running.
ai-e4.realWorld: Missing semicolon → red squiggle → add `;` → compiles.

ai-e5.title: A broken Java program and ask it to find the bug.
ai-e5.answer: Find bugs: read error line, check recent edits, print variables, fix one issue at a time. Logic bugs compile but give wrong output — trace step by step.
ai-e5.realWorld: NPE on null.length() — add null check before calling methods.

ai-e6.title: Compare: two AI responses for the same Java question.
ai-e6.answer: Compare AI answers: accurate? clear? compiles? Compare two responses — pick correct + simple over fancy + wrong.
ai-e6.realWorld: 'int always worse than double' — misleading. 'int for counts, double for prices' — correct.

ai-e7.title: Suggest better variable names for your code.
ai-e7.answer: Names describe purpose: studentCount not n, isLoggedIn not flag. camelCase in Java. Booleans often start with is/has.
ai-e7.realWorld: passingScore and grade beat x and s in code review.

ai-e8.title: Explain type casting with examples.
ai-e8.answer: Casting: int→double automatic. double→int needs (int) and drops decimals. Watch int division: 7/2 is 3, 7/2.0 is 3.5.
ai-e8.realWorld: (int)(29.97/2) = 14 — know when losing decimals is OK.

ai-e9.title: Create a quiz on Java basics.
ai-e9.answer: Self-quiz: main signature? 4 primitives? javac vs java? Fix `String name = Alex`. What is bytecode? == vs =?
ai-e9.realWorld: Cover answers, write from memory, then check — best prep for tests and interviews.

ai-e10.title: Review your Student Information System project and suggest improvements.
ai-e10.answer: Project review: meaningful names, input validation, no magic numbers, small methods, test empty list and bad input.
ai-e10.realWorld: Clean GitHub README + validation shows you think like a developer, not a copy-paster.

ai-w2-e1.title: Explain if-else with real-life examples.
ai-w2-e1.answer: Ask AI to explain if-else with a real scenario (ticket pricing, grades). Then write your own version without looking.
ai-w2-e1.realWorld: Prompt: 'Explain if-else using movie ticket discount for seniors and students. Java only, with println.'

ai-w2-e2.title: Explain loops visually.
ai-w2-e2.answer: Ask AI to draw a loop as a table: iteration, i, output. Redraw the table by hand to verify.
ai-w2-e2.realWorld: Visual trace of a for-loop printing stars — catches off-by-one errors early.

ai-w2-e3.title: Dry-run a Fibonacci program.
ai-w2-e3.answer: Dry-run Fibonacci: track a, b each iteration on paper. Compare with AI's trace. Mismatch = learning moment.
ai-w2-e3.realWorld: Interviewers ask dry-runs — AI is practice partner, not answer sheet.
ai-w2-e3.code:
int a=0,b=1;
for(int i=0;i<5;i++){ System.out.print(a+" "); int n=a+b; a=b; b=n; }

ai-w2-e4.title: Explain recursion without code (preview only).
ai-w2-e4.answer: Ask AI to explain recursion as 'function calling itself until base case' — no code yet. Draw the call stack on paper.
ai-w2-e4.realWorld: Preview before Week 8+ recursion deep-dive: factorial as 5! = 5 × 4!.

ai-w2-e5.title: Optimize a nested loop.
ai-w2-e5.answer: Paste nested loop finding duplicates. Ask for O(n) approach. Implement both; compare on array size 1000 vs 100000.
ai-w2-e5.realWorld: Measure, don't guess — AI's Big-O advice is theory until you time it.

ai-w2-e6.title: A program with logical bugs and identify them yourself before checking its answer.
ai-w2-e6.answer: Find bugs yourself first (15 min). Then ask AI. Compare solutions — did you miss the same bug?
ai-w2-e6.realWorld: Logic bug: loop uses <= length causing ArrayIndexOutOfBounds — classic AI and human catch.

ai-w2-e7.title: Create five loop practice problems.
ai-w2-e7.answer: Ask AI for 5 loop problems at your level. Solve without solution first. Use AI only for hints.
ai-w2-e7.realWorld: Generating practice beats passive reading — you need reps, not paragraphs.

ai-w2-e8.title: Generate edge cases for a calculator program.
ai-w2-e8.answer: Calculator edge cases: divide by zero, overflow, empty input, invalid operator. List them, then write tests.
ai-w2-e8.realWorld: AI lists cases; you own the test code — that's job-ready behavior.

ai-w2-e9.title: Review your code for readability.
ai-w2-e9.answer: Paste messy loop code. Ask: 'rename variables and add one comment per block.' Apply changes line by line.
ai-w2-e9.realWorld: Readability refactors are safe first AI-assisted edits — behavior stays same.

ai-w2-e10.title: Explain why your program is slow.
ai-w2-e10.answer: Paste slow code. Ask why it's slow (complexity, repeated work). Fix one issue and re-time.
ai-w2-e10.realWorld: Nested loop + repeated array scan — AI points to HashMap; you implement and verify.

ai-w3-e1.title: Review your Student class.
ai-w3-e1.answer: Paste Student class. Ask: encapsulation, naming, constructor issues. Fix top 2 findings yourself.
ai-w3-e1.realWorld: Public fields → private + getters is the most common Week 3 win.

ai-w3-e2.title: Suggest better variable names.
ai-w3-e2.answer: List all single-letter variables in your file. Ask AI for better names. Rename in IDE (refactor tool).
ai-w3-e2.realWorld: IDE rename is safer than AI find-replace — catches references.

ai-w3-e3.title: Explain why encapsulation is useful.
ai-w3-e3.answer: Ask AI why encapsulation matters — then explain aloud without reading. Add private balance + deposit() to a class.
ai-w3-e3.realWorld: Interview classic: 'Why private fields?' — control validation in one place.

ai-w3-e4.title: Generate a UML diagram from your classes.
ai-w3-e4.answer: List your classes and fields. Ask for PlantUML or text UML. Draw correct version by hand.
ai-w3-e4.realWorld: Wrong multiplicity on diagram → wrong DB schema later.

ai-w3-e5.title: Identify duplicate code.
ai-w3-e5.answer: Ask AI to find duplicate code blocks. Extract one shared method. Run tests.
ai-w3-e5.realWorld: Copy-pasted validation in 3 methods → one validateAge() helper.

ai-w3-e6.title: Suggest improvements for constructors.
ai-w3-e6.answer: Paste constructors. Ask for improvements: validation, chaining, defaults. Apply one fix.
ai-w3-e6.realWorld: Constructor setting negative age — add guard clause.

ai-w3-e7.title: Create JavaDoc comments.
ai-w3-e7.answer: Generate JavaDoc for public methods. Edit wrong descriptions. Match team style (@param, @return).
ai-w3-e7.realWorld: JavaDoc is contract for API consumers — worth getting right on libraries.

ai-w3-e8.title: Find design mistakes.
ai-w3-e8.answer: Ask: 'What design mistakes do you see?' — god class, tight coupling, static abuse. Pick one to fix.
ai-w3-e8.realWorld: Utility class with 20 static methods — split by domain.

ai-w3-e9.title: Compare: two class designs.
ai-w3-e9.answer: Describe two designs; ask AI to compare. You choose with reasoning — AI doesn't know your deadlines.
ai-w3-e9.realWorld: Inheritance vs composition for Employee types — trade-off discussion for interviews.

ai-w3-e10.title: Explain SOLID principles at a beginner level.
ai-w3-e10.answer: Ask for SOLID in simple terms with Java examples. Explain one principle without notes.
ai-w3-e10.realWorld: Single Responsibility: UserService saves users; EmailService sends mail — not one class doing both.

ai-w4-e1.title: Explain a NullPointerException.
ai-w4-e1.answer: Trigger NPE in a small program. Paste trace. Ask AI to explain each frame. Fix with null check.
ai-w4-e1.realWorld: String s = null; s.length() — classic first NPE lesson.

ai-w4-e2.title: Ask AI why ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException occurs.
ai-w4-e2.answer: Ask why ArrayIndexOutOfBounds happens. Relate to loop condition using array.length incorrectly.
ai-w4-e2.realWorld: for (i=0; i<=arr.length; i++) — should be i < arr.length.

ai-w4-e3.title: An exception stack trace and understand every line.
ai-w4-e3.answer: Paste full stack trace. Label: crash site, root cause, your fix line.
ai-w4-e3.realWorld: Reading traces calmly is a skill — practice on homework bugs before on-call.

ai-w4-e4.title: Refactor duplicated code.
ai-w4-e4.answer: Find duplicated if/else blocks. Ask AI to extract method. Verify output unchanged.
ai-w4-e4.realWorld: Same validation in add() and update() → validateStudent() once.

ai-w4-e5.title: Improve inheritance design.
ai-w4-e5.answer: Describe inheritance hierarchy. Ask if design fits Liskov. Simplify if subclass breaks parent contract.
ai-w4-e5.realWorld: Square extends Rectangle with setWidth breaking area — classic design smell.

ai-w4-e6.title: Compare: interface vs abstract class.
ai-w4-e6.answer: Ask: interface vs abstract class for your scenario. Build tiny example of each.
ai-w4-e6.realWorld: Multiple inheritance of type → interface. Shared base code → abstract class.

ai-w4-e7.title: Generate JavaDoc comments.
ai-w4-e7.answer: Generate JavaDoc for one class. Open in IDE preview. Fix errors.
ai-w4-e7.realWorld: Good JavaDoc shows in IDE hover — teammates read it daily.

ai-w4-e8.title: Explain SOLID principles with simple examples.
ai-w4-e8.answer: SOLID with examples: one principle per day. Single Responsibility first — split a fat class.
ai-w4-e8.realWorld: Interview: name SOLID letters and one Java example each.

ai-w4-e9.title: Suggest cleaner package structures.
ai-w4-e9.answer: List your packages. Ask if structure is clear. Move one misplaced class.
ai-w4-e9.realWorld: model classes in controller package — move to model/ for standard layout.

ai-w4-e10.title: Review your complete mini project.
ai-w4-e10.answer: Full project review: bugs, structure, naming. Fix highest-impact issue before marking done.
ai-w4-e10.realWorld: Portfolio project with one solid README + clean package structure impresses recruiters.

ai-w5-e1.title: Ask AI which collection should be used for different scenarios.
ai-w5-e1.answer: Describe 3 scenarios (lookup, sort, queue). Ask which Java collection. Justify each answer.
ai-w5-e1.realWorld: LRU cache → LinkedHashMap; priority tasks → PriorityQueue.

ai-w5-e2.title: Compare: ArrayList vs LinkedList using AI.
ai-w5-e2.answer: Compare ArrayList vs LinkedList with AI. Write micro-benchmark for get(5000) on 10k list.
ai-w5-e2.realWorld: ArrayList wins random access — measure proves textbook claim.

ai-w5-e3.title: Compare: HashMap vs TreeMap.
ai-w5-e3.answer: HashMap vs TreeMap: when need sorted keys? Write 5-line example of each.
ai-w5-e3.realWorld: TreeMap for range queries on keys; HashMap for plain speed.

ai-w5-e4.title: Explain HashSet internally.
ai-w5-e4.answer: Ask how HashSet avoids duplicates internally (hashCode, equals). Implement contains duplicate check both ways.
ai-w5-e4.realWorld: Understanding equals/hashCode contract prevents Set bugs with custom objects.

ai-w5-e5.title: Review your Student Management System.
ai-w5-e5.answer: Review Student Management System for collection misuse. Fix one hot path.
ai-w5-e5.realWorld: Linear search in list on every login → Map by studentId.

ai-w5-e6.title: Optimize nested loops using collections.
ai-w5-e6.answer: Replace nested loop duplicate-finder with HashSet. Same results, time both.
ai-w5-e6.realWorld: Classic optimization exercise — appears in interviews and real code review.

ai-w5-e7.title: Explain Big O notation with examples.
ai-w5-e7.answer: Ask Big-O of your methods. Explain n² loop aloud in 30 seconds.
ai-w5-e7.realWorld: Interview: 'What's complexity of this nested for?' — practice here.

ai-w5-e8.title: Generate edge test cases.
ai-w5-e8.answer: List edge tests for a method using collections. Implement 3 as JUnit or main asserts.
ai-w5-e8.realWorld: Empty list, null list, list with null element — define expected behavior.

ai-w5-e9.title: Suggest better collection choices.
ai-w5-e9.answer: Paste code using Vector or Hashtable. Ask for modern alternative. Migrate if safe.
ai-w5-e9.realWorld: Legacy synchronized collections → ArrayList + Collections.synchronizedList or concurrent types.

ai-w5-e10.title: Explain memory usage of different collections.
ai-w5-e10.answer: Ask memory difference: ArrayList of 1M Integers vs int[]. Discuss boxing cost.
ai-w5-e10.realWorld: Production lesson: primitives in tight memory paths matter at scale.

ai-w6-e1.title: Convert a for loop into a Stream.
ai-w6-e1.answer: Convert for-loop filtering list to stream().filter().collect(). Same output, compare readability.
ai-w6-e1.realWorld: Team code review often debates loop vs Stream — know both.

ai-w6-e2.title: Explain a complex Lambda Expression.
ai-w6-e2.answer: Paste complex lambda. Ask line-by-line explanation. Rewrite in plain loop.
ai-w6-e2.realWorld: If you can't read it as a loop, don't ship it.

ai-w6-e3.title: Compare: Streams vs Loops.
ai-w6-e3.answer: Compare Streams vs loops: debugging, performance, readability. When would you choose each?
ai-w6-e3.realWorld: Breakpoints in loops are easier for juniors — valid reason to prefer loops.

ai-w6-e4.title: Optimize collection processing.
ai-w6-e4.answer: Optimize list processing: remove repeated stream passes. Combine filter+map in one pipeline.
ai-w6-e4.realWorld: Three separate streams over same list → one pipeline, one pass.

ai-w6-e5.title: Explain synchronization visually.
ai-w6-e5.answer: Ask visual explanation of synchronized block. Draw two threads and lock.
ai-w6-e5.realWorld: Only one thread inside synchronized method at a time — mutex concept.

ai-w6-e6.title: Identify race conditions.
ai-w6-e6.answer: Write buggy shared counter. Ask AI why race happens. Fix with synchronization or AtomicInteger.
ai-w6-e6.realWorld: Classic interview: why counter++ is not atomic.

ai-w6-e7.title: Improve thread performance.
ai-w6-e7.answer: ExecutorService: submit tasks, shutdown, awaitTermination. Ask for thread pool sizing basics.
ai-w6-e7.realWorld: Fixed pool of 4 for 4-core CPU — starting point, not law.

ai-w6-e8.title: Review Stream code readability.
ai-w6-e8.answer: Review Stream code for readability. Split long pipeline or add method references.

ai-w6-e9.title: Explain Executor Service with a real-world analogy.
ai-w6-e9.answer: Ask ExecutorService analogy (workers, queue). Implement 3 tasks printing thread name.
ai-w6-e9.realWorld: Thread pool = limited workers pulling jobs from queue — factory floor model.

ai-w6-e10.title: Generate test cases for multithreaded code.
ai-w6-e10.answer: Generate concurrent test scenarios. Expect flakiness — use CountDownLatch for coordination.
ai-w6-e10.realWorld: Multithreaded tests are hard — AI gives ideas; you stabilize with proper sync.

ai-w7-e1.title: Design a Student database.
ai-w7-e1.answer: Design Student DB: tables, PKs, FKs. Ask AI, then draw your own ER diagram and compare.
ai-w7-e1.realWorld: student, course, enrollment — standard academic schema pattern.

ai-w7-e2.title: Generate SQL tables for a Library system.
ai-w7-e2.answer: Generate Library CREATE TABLE scripts. Run in Docker Postgres. Fix type errors.
ai-w7-e2.realWorld: book_copy_status ENUM or VARCHAR — pick one, document in README.

ai-w7-e3.title: Explain INNER JOIN with examples.
ai-w7-e3.answer: Ask INNER JOIN explanation with 3-row example. Write join query yourself, verify row count.
ai-w7-e3.realWorld: Only matching rows appear — unmatched books without loans excluded.

ai-w7-e4.title: Optimize a slow SQL query.
ai-w7-e4.answer: Paste slow query. Run EXPLAIN. Ask AI for rewrite. Compare execution time.
ai-w7-e4.realWorld: Subquery in SELECT → JOIN often faster — measure both.

ai-w7-e5.title: Review your JDBC code.
ai-w7-e5.answer: Paste JDBC connection + query code. Ask for try-with-resources and PreparedStatement fixes.
ai-w7-e5.realWorld: Leak from unclosed Connection — try-with-resources standard fix.

ai-w7-e6.title: Generate JUnit test cases.
ai-w7-e6.answer: Ask JUnit ideas for a DAO. Implement 2 tests with H2 in-memory DB.
ai-w7-e6.realWorld: @DataJpaTest or plain JDBC test — isolate DB layer.

ai-w7-e7.title: Explain SQL normalization.
ai-w7-e7.answer: Ask 1NF, 2NF, 3NF with one example each. Find one violation in a denormalized table you design.
ai-w7-e7.realWorld: Repeating course name on every enrollment row → normalize to course table.

ai-w7-e8.title: Create an ER diagram for your project.
ai-w7-e8.answer: Describe project entities; get ER from AI. Redraw correctly for portfolio README.
ai-w7-e8.realWorld: Visual ER in README helps recruiters understand your capstone in 10 seconds.

ai-w7-e9.title: Suggest indexes for better performance.
ai-w7-e9.answer: List your frequent WHERE/JOIN columns. Ask which indexes help. Create one, re-EXPLAIN.
ai-w7-e9.realWorld: Index on foreign keys used in JOINs — common win.

ai-w7-e10.title: Identify SQL injection vulnerabilities and show how PreparedStatement prevents them.
ai-w7-e10.answer: Show vulnerable string concat SQL. Ask AI to fix with PreparedStatement. Explain injection in one sentence.
ai-w7-e10.realWorld: '; DROP TABLE users; -- classic — parameters prevent it.
ai-w7-e10.code:
PreparedStatement ps = conn.prepareStatement("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?");
ps.setLong(1, userId);

ai-w8-e1.title: Generate API documentation.
ai-w8-e1.answer: Paste controller code. Ask for OpenAPI-style doc. Compare with springdoc output.
ai-w8-e1.realWorld: Accurate docs = fewer Slack questions from frontend.

ai-w8-e2.title: Review your REST API design.
ai-w8-e2.answer: Describe your API. Ask REST design review: nouns, verbs, status codes. Fix one endpoint.

ai-w8-e3.title: Suggest better endpoint names.
ai-w8-e3.answer: List endpoints. Ask for better names and URI structure. Apply one rename with deprecation plan.

ai-w8-e4.title: Explain HTTP status codes.
ai-w8-e4.answer: Ask when to use 200, 201, 400, 401, 404, 500. Map each to your API scenario.
ai-w8-e4.realWorld: 201 + Location header on create; 404 when student id not found.

ai-w8-e5.title: Generate Postman test cases.
ai-w8-e5.answer: Generate Postman tests for login + protected route. Import and run.
ai-w8-e5.realWorld: Test script stores JWT from login, uses in next request header.

ai-w8-e6.title: Generate JSON request bodies.
ai-w8-e6.answer: Ask sample JSON request bodies for your DTOs. Validate against @Valid annotations.
ai-w8-e6.realWorld: Missing required field → 400 with field errors — test that case.

ai-w8-e7.title: Create API validation rules.
ai-w8-e7.answer: List validation rules AI suggests: email format, password length, @NotNull. Add to DTO.

ai-w8-e8.title: Review REST best practices.
ai-w8-e8.answer: REST checklist: stateless, cache headers, HATEOAS optional, consistent errors. Score your API.
ai-w8-e8.realWorld: Same error JSON shape on all 400s — {code, message, field}.

ai-w8-e9.title: Identify security issues in an API.
ai-w8-e9.answer: Paste API with hardcoded secrets or no auth. Ask security review. Fix findings.
ai-w8-e9.realWorld: API key in query string → move to Authorization header.

ai-w8-e10.title: Explain REST architecture with real-world examples.
ai-w8-e10.answer: Ask REST architecture: client, gateway, service, DB. Draw for your project.
ai-w8-e10.realWorld: Mobile → API Gateway → Spring Boot → PostgreSQL — interview whiteboard staple.

ai-w9-e1.title: Review your Spring Security configuration.
ai-w9-e1.answer: Paste SecurityConfig. Ask what's missing: CSRF, CORS, permitAll too broad. Fix one issue.
ai-w9-e1.realWorld: /admin/** permitAll by mistake — lock to ROLE_ADMIN.

ai-w9-e2.title: Identify security vulnerabilities in your API.
ai-w9-e2.answer: Ask security review of your API: auth on all routes?, SQL injection?, mass assignment?
ai-w9-e2.realWorld: Binding entity directly from request → DTO + validation instead.

ai-w9-e3.title: Explain JWT token flow step by step.
ai-w9-e3.answer: Ask JWT flow: login → token → Authorization header → filter validates → SecurityContext.
ai-w9-e3.realWorld: Once you can draw the flow, Spring Security config makes sense.

ai-w9-e4.title: Compare: JWT vs Sessions.
ai-w9-e4.answer: Compare JWT vs server sessions: stateless vs stateful, logout, scale. Pick for your app type.
ai-w9-e4.realWorld: SPA + mobile often JWT; traditional web app may use session cookies.

ai-w9-e5.title: Generate secure password validation rules.
ai-w9-e5.answer: Generate password rules: length, upper, digit, special. Implement in validator + test.

ai-w9-e6.title: Review your login API.
ai-w9-e6.answer: Review login endpoint: rate limit?, generic error messages?, BCrypt?
ai-w9-e6.realWorld: 'Invalid credentials' not 'User not found' — prevents user enumeration.

ai-w9-e7.title: Explain BCrypt hashing.
ai-w9-e7.answer: BCrypt hashes passwords one-way with salt. Never store plain text. Spring: BCryptPasswordEncoder.
ai-w9-e7.realWorld: passwordEncoder.encode(raw) on register; matches() on login.

ai-w9-e8.title: Suggest secure REST API practices.
ai-w9-e8.answer: Secure REST checklist: HTTPS, auth, validation, rate limit, CORS whitelist, no secrets in repo.
ai-w9-e8.realWorld: Score your project; fix top gap before portfolio demo.

ai-w9-e9.title: Create authentication flow diagrams.
ai-w9-e9.answer: Ask for auth flow diagram: register, login, refresh, protected call. Draw and label components.
ai-w9-e9.realWorld: Diagram in README shows security thinking to interviewers.

ai-w9-e10.title: Identify common authentication mistakes.
ai-w9-e10.answer: Common mistakes: JWT in localStorage XSS risk, long-lived tokens, no HTTPS, default admin password.
ai-w9-e10.realWorld: httpOnly cookie for token — discuss trade-offs with AI, decide consciously.

ai-w10-e1.title: Generate OpenAPI/Swagger documentation for your API.
ai-w10-e1.answer: Add springdoc to Spring Boot. Generate OpenAPI. Export JSON to repo.
ai-w10-e1.realWorld: CI fails if undocumented endpoint added — optional advanced step.

ai-w10-e2.title: Create a Postman collection with test scripts.
ai-w10-e2.answer: Build Postman collection with tests for 3 endpoints. Share export.json in repo.

ai-w10-e3.title: Review your REST API endpoint naming and URI design.
ai-w10-e3.answer: Review URI design: plural nouns, no verbs in path, nested resources max 2 levels.

ai-w10-e4.title: Explain when to use 400 vs 422 vs 409 status codes.
ai-w10-e4.answer: 400 bad request (malformed), 422 unprocessable (valid JSON, business rule fail), 409 conflict (duplicate).

ai-w10-e5.title: Suggest pagination and filtering query parameters.
ai-w10-e5.answer: Add ?page=0&size=20&sort=name,asc. Document in OpenAPI. AI suggests defaults.
ai-w10-e5.realWorld: Pageable in Spring: PageRequest.of(page, size, Sort.by("name")).

ai-w10-e6.title: Review your DTO and mapper pattern implementation.
ai-w10-e6.answer: Review Entity→DTO mapping. Ask if MapStruct or manual mapper is cleaner. Avoid exposing entities.
ai-w10-e6.realWorld: UserDTO without password hash — security + stable API contract.

ai-w10-e7.title: Identify missing validation rules in your API.
ai-w10-e7.answer: Scan DTOs for missing @NotNull, @Email, @Size. Add validation messages.
ai-w10-e7.realWorld: Clear message: 'Email must be valid' helps frontend show errors.

ai-w10-e8.title: Generate API test cases for edge cases.
ai-w10-e8.answer: Edge API tests: empty body, huge payload, wrong Content-Type, expired token.
ai-w10-e8.realWorld: 413 or 400 on 10MB JSON when limit is 1MB — define and test.

ai-w10-e9.title: Suggest rate limiting and CORS configuration.
ai-w10-e9.answer: Rate limiting (Bucket4j, gateway) and CORS allowed origins — AI suggests config snippets.
ai-w10-e9.realWorld: CORS * in prod is risky — whitelist frontend domain only.

ai-w10-e10.title: Optimize your API response structure for production.
ai-w10-e10.answer: Trim API responses: no internal ids leak, pagination meta, HATEOAS links optional.
ai-w10-e10.realWorld: {data:[], page:0, total:100} — consistent envelope helps clients.

ai-w11-e1.title: Generate an ER diagram for a banking system.
ai-w11-e1.answer: Generate banking ER: Customer, Account, Transaction. Draw and implement 3 Flyway scripts.
ai-w11-e1.realWorld: Portfolio piece: schema + ER image in README.

ai-w11-e2.title: Design a normalized schema for an e-commerce platform.
ai-w11-e2.answer: E-commerce schema to 3NF. Identify duplicate product name on order lines — normalize.

ai-w11-e3.title: Write a complex SQL query with CTEs and window functions.
ai-w11-e3.answer: Write CTE query: top 3 products by revenue per category. Run and verify counts.
ai-w11-e3.realWorld: Window functions — common data interview question.

ai-w11-e4.title: Optimize a slow query using EXPLAIN plan analysis.
ai-w11-e4.answer: Slow query + EXPLAIN. Apply index or rewrite. Document before/after ms.
ai-w11-e4.realWorld: Proof in README: 'Query 2s → 40ms with index on status, created_at.'

ai-w11-e5.title: Recommend indexes for your most-used queries.
ai-w11-e5.answer: List top 5 queries from your app. Ask index recommendations. Add one, measure.
ai-w11-e5.realWorld: Partial index WHERE status = 'ACTIVE' — smaller, faster for hot path.

ai-w11-e6.title: Debug an N+1 query problem in Hibernate.
ai-w11-e6.answer: Reproduce N+1: log SQL count in test. Fix with @EntityGraph or JOIN FETCH.
ai-w11-e6.realWorld: 1 + N queries → 1 query — dramatic improvement on list endpoints.

ai-w11-e7.title: Generate MongoDB aggregation pipeline queries.
ai-w11-e7.answer: MongoDB aggregation for monthly sales. Import sample docs, run pipeline.

ai-w11-e8.title: Create Flyway migration scripts for your schema.
ai-w11-e8.answer: Flyway V1 baseline + V2 add column migration. Run on clean DB.
ai-w11-e8.realWorld: mvn flyway:migrate — same flow as team CI.

ai-w11-e9.title: Explain lazy vs eager loading trade-offs for your entities.
ai-w11-e9.answer: Lazy vs eager on @ManyToOne: default lazy, eager only when always needed. Explain trade-off for your entity.
ai-w11-e9.realWorld: Eager Order→Customer loads customers always — often N+1 or over-fetch.

ai-w11-e10.title: Review your database schema for normalization issues.
ai-w11-e10.answer: Review schema for denormalization without reason, missing FKs, nullable everything.
ai-w11-e10.realWorld: NOT NULL on required fields — data quality at DB layer.

ai-w12-e1.title: Build an embedding service with Spring AI and store vectors in pgvector
ai-w12-e1.answer: Build embedding service: Spring AI EmbeddingModel → save vectors in pgvector. Ingest 10 text chunks.
ai-w12-e1.realWorld: Verify with SELECT count(*) FROM vector_store; similarity query returns relevant chunk.

ai-w12-e2.title: Implement a basic RAG chatbot over your project documentation
ai-w12-e2.answer: RAG chatbot: user question → retrieve top-k chunks → prompt with context → answer. Cite source doc name.
ai-w12-e2.realWorld: Ask 'How do I run tests?' — answer must quote your README, not hallucinate.

ai-w12-e3.title: Add function calling so the LLM can query a Spring Data repository
ai-w12-e3.answer: Function calling: LLM invokes method to fetch live data from Spring Data repo (e.g. order by id).
ai-w12-e3.realWorld: Question 'status of order 42' → tool call → DB → natural language reply.

ai-w12-e4.title: Stream chat responses to the browser using SSE in Spring Boot
ai-w12-e4.answer: Stream ChatClient response via SSE endpoint. Browser shows tokens as they arrive.
ai-w12-e4.realWorld: WebFlux Flux<String> or SseEmitter — pick what matches your stack.

ai-w12-e5.title: Load PDF documents with LangChain4j and answer questions about them
ai-w12-e5.answer: LangChain4j: load PDF from resources, split, embed, answer questions about content.
ai-w12-e5.realWorld: Compare DX with Spring AI on same PDF — note preferences.

ai-w12-e6.title: Integrate an MCP tool that exposes your REST API to an AI agent
ai-w12-e6.answer: MCP or tool wrapper exposing one read-only REST endpoint to an agent. Document setup.
ai-w12-e6.realWorld: Even a minimal tool teaches protocol thinking for future IDE integrations.

ai-w12-e7.title: Implement token-budget middleware to cap per-request LLM cost
ai-w12-e7.answer: Middleware rejects requests exceeding token budget (estimate prompt length). Return 429 with clear message.
ai-w12-e7.realWorld: Prevent runaway costs from huge pasted logs.

ai-w12-e8.title: Unit-test an AI wrapper service with Mockito and WireMock
ai-w12-e8.answer: Unit test AiService with @MockBean ChatModel. Assert prompt includes retrieved context string.
ai-w12-e8.realWorld: No network in CI — fast, deterministic tests.

ai-w12-e9.title: Create a golden-dataset eval for prompt consistency
ai-w12-e9.answer: Golden dataset: 10 question/expected_source pairs. Script scores retrieval hit rate.
ai-w12-e9.realWorld: Regression when you change chunk size — eval catches quality drop.

ai-w12-e10.title: Dockerize an AI microservice with health checks and secrets
ai-w12-e10.answer: Dockerfile: JRE, non-root user, health check /actuator/health, secrets from env. Push to registry.
ai-w12-e10.realWorld: Same container discipline as non-AI microservices.

ai-w12-e11.title: Add OpenTelemetry spans around LLM and embedding calls
ai-w12-e11.answer: OpenTelemetry span around chat() and embed() with attributes for model and duration.
ai-w12-e11.realWorld: Jaeger trace shows LLM call as 80% latency — focus optimization there.

ai-w12-e12.title: Build a simple cost dashboard from logged token usage
ai-w12-e12.answer: Log tokens per request to DB or metrics. Simple dashboard: daily cost estimate.
ai-w12-e12.realWorld: product_owner sees $/day — informs model downgrade decisions.

ai-w12-e13.title: Implement hybrid search combining keyword and vector retrieval
ai-w12-e13.answer: Hybrid search: BM25 keyword + vector merge scores. Improves exact SKU/code lookup.
ai-w12-e13.realWorld: Query 'ERR-4521' needs keyword; 'refund process' needs semantic.

ai-w12-e14.title: Build a multi-tool agent that plans and executes Java service calls
ai-w12-e14.answer: Multi-tool agent: plan → call weather API tool → call calendar tool → summarize. Cap steps.
ai-w12-e14.realWorld: maxIterations=5 prevents infinite agent loops and cost blowup.

ai-w12-e15.title: Capstone: deploy a production-ready RAG API with tests and CI
ai-w12-e15.answer: Capstone: RAG API with tests, eval, Docker, CI, README architecture diagram. Deploy optional.
ai-w12-e15.realWorld: This project is your Gen AI interview centerpiece — polish README and demo video.
